title: How to Find Ideas 〜Turn Problems into Treasure〜
description: Let's have fun learning how to find ideas with Gal-chan!

scenes.speech: Where are business ideas hiding? Let's go find them together!
scenes.title: How to Find Ideas 〜Turn Problems into Treasure〜

scenes.speech: Let's learn how to find ideas with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: The Source of Ideas: 'Dissatisfaction'
scenes.content: The feeling of 'this is inconvenient' or 'why is it like this?' is the seed of business. Uber was born from 'I can't get a taxi!' and Airbnb from 'hotel prices are too high!'. Your dissatisfaction has value!
scenes.highlight: Uber's Birth: On a night when taxis couldn't be hailed, the idea for 'an app that can call a taxi instantly' was born

scenes.speech: Let's learn how to find ideas with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: The 5W1H Analysis: Finding Problems
scenes.content: Why (why is it like this?), What (what's the problem?), Who (who's struggling?), When (when does it happen?), Where (where does it happen?), How (how to solve it?) - analyzing everyday events with these 6 questions reveals business opportunities!
scenes.highlight: 5W1H Thinking: A universal framework for discovering and solving all business challenges

scenes.speech: Let's learn how to find ideas with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: Find the 'Pain Points'!
scenes.content: The points where customers feel pain are business opportunities. ① Inconvenient ② Takes too much time ③ Too expensive ④ Poor quality ⑤ No options - finding these 5 pain points leads to treasure!
scenes.highlight: Pain Point Discovery Method: Just ask people around you 'what have you been struggling with lately?' and a mountain of treasure appears!

scenes.speech: Let's learn how to find ideas with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: Understanding True Needs through Interviews
scenes.content: When asked 'would you use a service like this?', people often say 'yes'. But true needs are revealed from past actions: 'when did you last struggle with this?' and 'what did you do then?'
scenes.highlight: The Golden Rule of Customer Interviews: 'what do you really need?' rather than 'do you want a service like this?'

scenes.speech: Let's learn how to find ideas with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: Observation is the Best Research Method
scenes.content: Observe what people struggle with at the library, what sells at stores, and what's trending on social media - 'field observation (ethnography)' is a treasure trove of business ideas!
scenes.highlight: Design Thinking: 'observation, empathy, definition, idea generation, prototyping, testing' - a problem-solving method used by top companies worldwide

scenes.speech: Let's learn how to find ideas with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: Reading the Future from Trends
scenes.content: Trends, technological trends, and social changes - predicting 'what's next' from these is 'trend analysis'. AI, aging population, climate change, and local migration - businesses that ride big trends are more likely to succeed!
scenes.highlight: 'Ride the trend': riding growth trends is the easiest way to expand your business

scenes.speech: Let's learn how to find ideas with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: Explosive Idea Generation with Brainstorming
scenes.content: More people generate more ideas. Rules: ① no criticism ② quantity over quality ③ any crazy idea is okay ④ build on others' ideas - try to generate 100 ideas in 15 minutes with these rules!
scenes.highlight: The Effect of Brainstorming: idea quantity increases 3-5 times in a criticism-free environment

scenes.speech: Let's learn how to find ideas with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: The 9 Elements of a Business Model (BMC)
scenes.content: Business Model Canvas (BMC): ① customer segments ② value proposition ③ channels ④ customer relationships ⑤ revenue streams ⑥ resources ⑦ activities ⑧ partners ⑨ cost structure - summarizing these 9 elements on one sheet reveals your business!
scenes.highlight: BMC (Business Model Canvas): developed by Alex Osterwalder, used by startups worldwide

scenes.speech: Mastered idea discovery! Next, create an MVP 🎉
scenes.title: Lesson Complete!
